title: BRIGHT: A Realistic and Challenging Benchmark for Reasoning-Intensive Retrieval
authors: Hongjin Su; Howard Yen; Mengzhou Xia; Weijia Shi; Niklas Muennighoff; Han-yu Wang; Haisu Liu; Quan Shi; Zachary S. Siegel; Michael Tang; Ruoxi Sun; Jinsung Yoon; Sercan O. Arik; Danqi Chen; Tao Yu
abstract: We introduce BRIGHT a benchmark for retrieval tasks that require reasoning to resolve semantic differences between queries and documents. Existing benchmarks largely fail to capture the difficulty of real-world retrieval which often requires multi-step reasoning to bridge lexical and semantic gaps.

title: Scaling Sparse and Dense Retrieval to Billions of Documents
authors: Carlos Lassance; Hervé Déjean; Stéphane Clinchant
abstract: We study the scalability of sparse and dense retrieval methods when indexing billions of documents and propose hybrid approaches that balance precision and efficiency.

title: FIRST: Faster Improved Listwise Reranking with Single Token Decoding
authors: Revanth Gangi Reddy; JaeHyeok Doo; Yifei Xu; Md Arafat Sultan; Deevya Swain; Avirup Sil; Heng Ji
abstract: We propose FIRST a listwise reranking approach that uses a single token decoding step to score documents enabling significantly faster reranking without sacrificing effectiveness.

title: Discrete Prompt Optimization for Plausible-but-Inconsistent Claims in Retrieval-Augmented Generation
authors: Xiang Chen; Duyu Tang; Xiaocheng Feng; Lei Li; Bing Qin
abstract: We address the problem of plausible-but-inconsistent claims generated by RAG systems and propose a discrete prompt optimization method to improve faithfulness.

title: Cross-Modal Retrieval with Semantic Alignment for E-Commerce
authors: Lin Jiang; Pengcheng Wei; Liang Wang; Fuli Feng; Xiangnan He
abstract: We propose a cross-modal retrieval framework that aligns visual and textual semantics for product search in e-commerce settings.

title: HyDE+: Improving Hypothetical Document Embeddings with Query Decomposition
abstract: We extend HyDE by decomposing complex queries into sub-queries before generating hypothetical documents improving retrieval performance on multi-hop questions.

title: Adaptive Sparse Retrieval via Dynamic Term Weighting
authors: Thibault Formal; Benjamin Piwowarski; Stéphane Clinchant
abstract: We propose an adaptive sparse retrieval method that dynamically adjusts term weights based on query context achieving competitive performance with dense retrieval.

title: Graph-Enhanced Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Complex QA
authors: Jinhao Jiang; Kun Zhou; Zican Dong; Keming Ye; Wayne Xin Zhao; Ji-Rong Wen
abstract: We propose a graph-enhanced RAG framework that leverages entity relationships to retrieve and reason over multi-hop questions in open-domain QA.

title: Efficient Web-Scale Dense Retrieval with Approximate Nearest Neighbor Search
authors: Han Zhang; Madian Khabsa; Mike Lewis
abstract: We study efficient ANN methods for web-scale dense retrieval and propose optimizations that reduce index memory while preserving retrieval quality.

title: RankRAG: Unifying Context Ranking with Retrieval-Augmented Generation
authors: Yue Yu; Wei Ping; Zihan Liu; Boxin Wang; Jiaxuan You; Chao Zhang; Mohammad Shoeybi; Bryan Catanzaro
abstract: We propose RankRAG a framework that jointly optimizes context ranking and answer generation in a unified RAG pipeline.

title: Improving Dense Retrieval with Position-Aware Embedding
authors: Tianhao Li; Patrick Lewis; Pontus Stenetorp
abstract: We introduce position-aware embeddings that encode token positions explicitly in bi-encoder models improving dense retrieval on long documents.

title: Contrastive Learning for Multi-Vector Dense Retrieval
authors: Sheng-Chieh Lin; Minghan Li; Jimmy Lin
abstract: We propose a contrastive learning objective tailored for multi-vector dense retrieval models that improves late-interaction scoring.

title: SELF-RAG: Learning to Retrieve Generate and Critique through Self-Reflection
authors: Akari Asai; Zeqiu Wu; Yizhong Wang; Avirup Sil; Hannaneh Hajishirzi
abstract: We train a model to adaptively retrieve passages on demand and generate and reflect on retrieved passages and its own generation using special tokens.

title: Embedding Compression for Efficient Dense Retrieval at Scale
authors: Tao Shen; Xiubo Geng; Guodong Long; Daxin Jiang
abstract: We propose learned embedding compression techniques that reduce the storage and compute cost of dense retrieval while maintaining retrieval effectiveness.

title: Improving Text Embeddings with Large Language Models
authors: Liang Wang; Nan Yang; Xiaolong Huang; Linjun Yang; Rangan Majumder; Furu Wei
abstract: We introduce a method for training high-quality text embeddings using synthetic data generated by large language models achieving strong zero-shot performance.

title: Retrieval Head Mechanistically Explains Long-Context Factuality
authors: Wenhao Wu; Yizhong Wang; Guangxuan Xiao; Hao Peng; Yao Fu
abstract: We identify retrieval heads in transformer models that are mechanistically responsible for retrieving and copying information from long contexts.
